scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Strong rule extraction (12 rules with individual confidence ratings) and 7 clarifying questions far exceed minimums. Demand is clearly identified as undefined (Ambiguity #1, Rule 9). However, three pass criteria are violated: (1) confidence.level is 65, exceeding the <=40 pass threshold and hitting the >=65 fail threshold; (2) Every rule includes a 'Testable Assertion' written in Given/When/Then format, directly violating the 'No Given/When/Then criteria written' requirement; (3) The 'Recommended Default' column invents specific values ('Max 2x base price', 'min = cost + 10% margin', 'max 20% change per 15min') without flagging them as invented. The analysis treats many rules as HIGH confidence invariants when the input is too vague to warrant that — Rules 2-6 are all marked HIGH/TRUE invariant, producing a domain analysis that reads as more complete than the input justifies. Saved from 'fail' by the extensive ambiguity surface area, heavy caveats in the bottom-line summary, and the fact that invented values are at least isolated in a 'Recommended Default' column rather than embedded in the rules themselves.

observations.text: Extracted 12 rules with individual confidence ratings — well above the 5-rule minimum
Surfaced 7 clarifying ambiguities in a structured table — exceeds the 5-question minimum
Demand identified as undefined critical gap in both Rule 9 and Ambiguity #1
Bottom-line summary correctly states ambiguities are blocking and implementation without resolution guarantees domain drift
Low-confidence rules (11, 12) appropriately flagged as inferred rather than stated
confidence.level of 65 hits the fail threshold (>=65) and far exceeds the pass ceiling of 40 — input this vague should not produce 65% confidence
Every rule includes a Given/When/Then-style 'Testable Assertion' — the rubric explicitly requires NO Given/When/Then criteria from this vague input
Recommended Defaults column invents specific values (2x multiplier, 10% margin, 20% change per 15min, top 3 competitors) without flagging them as invented

Rules 2-6 all rated HIGH confidence with TRUE invariant status, treating vague CEO statements as hard specifications — 'demand is high' and 'guardrails' are not HIGH confidence when demand and guardrail values are undefined
The analysis reads as substantially complete despite the input being substantially incomplete — a domain expert should have refused to produce testable assertions from this input

agent_output_excerpt:

domain_analysis:

    and last-mile delivery. The CEO's language reveals both explicit rules and
    implicit constraints that any faithful implementation must honor.
  ubiquitous_language:
    - term: DynaPricing
      definition: The bounded system r
